content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

September 20, 1995

Honorable Tim Leslie
Senator, First District
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-95-297

Dear Senator Leslie:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹  Since your advice request is general in nature, we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

QUESTION

Does the Act prohibit corporations from making campaign
contributions to the candidate controlled committees of state
legislative candidates?

CONCLUSION

The Act does not prohibit corporate contributions, nor does
it treat corporate contributions any differently than
contributions from individuals.

DISCUSSION

Section 89510 provides:

(a)  A candidate may only accept contributions
from persons, political committees, broad based

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-95-297
Page 2


political committees, and political parties and
only in the amounts specified in Article 3
(commencing with Section 85300). A candidate shall
not accept contributions from any other source.

Section 82047 defines "person" as individuals,
proprietorships, firms, partnerships, joint ventures, syndicates,
business trusts, companies, associations,
committees, and any other organizations or groups of persons
acting in concert.

Thus, the Act expressly permits corporate contributions to
state and local candidates. With respect to federal candidates,
the Federal Election Campaign Act controls.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
     Counsel, Legal Division
